summary: Trojan wrapper forces install of unrelated dependency @sqlite-node/createsql
details: @sql-access/nodesql@1.1.1 is a deceptive wrapper. Its index.js is a verbatim copy of the unrelated `buffer` module (Feross Aboukhadijeh, attribution preserved at index.js:2), with the only added line being a self-import `var ins = import('@sql-access/nodesql');` at index.js:11. The package.json declares a runtime dependency on `@sqlite-node/createsql ^1.0.5` (package.json:12) which is never imported or used by the code. Multiple inconsistent identities are present in a single tarball: the package name `@sql-access/nodesql`, README heading `bare-stream`, README install instruction `npm i @sql-access/nods`, dependency `@sqlite-node/createsql`, and code body cloned from `buffer`. The repository field points at `guilderguzman/sql-link`, matching none of these names. The package's only meaningful effect on installers is forcing resolution of `@sqlite-node/createsql` into the dependency tree on `npm install`, which is the classic vehicle-package shape: harmless-looking surface + a transitive that carries the actual payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
